'''Worrites''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Crustacean/Legends|crustacean]]–[[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] [[Hybrid/Legends|hybrids]] from an unknown [[Worrite homeworld|part]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. As tall as [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]], their anatomy was dominated by a knobby, protective carapace. Members of the species had four [[Limb/Legends|arms]], two of which served as small manipulators, and two ending in huge claws. A Worrite's face consisted of a mass of sensory feelers and four [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] on long [[Ommatophora|stalks]] that were able to swivel in all directions. The Worrite [[Mazumoda]], nicknamed "the big bug," left his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] and served as a [[Mercenary/Legends|thug-for-hire]] in the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|underworld]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]]. His frightening appearance and giant pincers served as assets in his line of work.

==Biology and appearance==
Worrites were a [[Hybrid/Legends|hybrid]] [[Species/Legends|species]] with both [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] and [[Crustacean/Legends|crustacean]] features present in roughly equal proportion. Their bodies were protected by a rugged [[exoskeleton]] that was divided into segments. In one member of the species, the carapace was colored a dark, dull [[Color/Legends|red]]. They had two small [[Limb/Legends|arms]] that they held close to the body and used for fine manipulation. Another two arms harbored gigantic clawed pincers. Closed, these claws were deadly bludgeoning weapons; however, when used as pincers, they could easily sever the limbs of most [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] species. Worrites were as tall as [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]]. The overall effect was enough to frighten many non-Worrites.<ref name="C&TCW" />

The Worrite visage was primarily composed of numerous feelers with which the species sensed the outside world. Four [[Ommatophora|stalks]] rose from the head, each supporting an [[Eye/Legends|eye]]. These eyestalks were flexible and allowed the species a wide field of vision. Worrites had two [[Gender/Legends|genders]]: male and female.<ref name="C&TCW" />

==Society and culture==
Worrites were capable of learning and speaking a wide variety of [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Language/Legends|languages]]. The Worrite [[Mazumoda]], for example, was a fluent speaker of [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]], [[Mugaar (language)|Mugaar]], and [[Rodese/Legends|Rodese]]. The species' pincer-like claws and large size made them frightening warriors who were able to both strike with blunt trauma and clip off limbs.<ref name="C&TCW" />

==History==
The Worrites hailed from an unknown [[Worrite homeworld|part]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="C&TCW" /> At some point before [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="time">According to ''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'', [[Mazumoda]] worked for [[Beuga Odell]], who was active during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], and for [[Quetemoor the Sub-Elder]], who had relations with [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] under the rule of [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]]. Xizor [[Death/Legends|died]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the Worrite Mazumoda made it off his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] and to the galactic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. There, he found a niche as a freelance bruiser, taking jobs for various [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|underworld]] figures, who dubbed him "the big bug." The [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lords]] [[Beuga Odell]] and [[Quetemoor the Sub-Elder]], for example, retained his services as a [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] and enforcer. Meanwhile, the bookmaker [[Tuchap]] employed him to intimidate clients late with their payments. His fearsome appearance and sound job performance earned Mazumoda offers of permanent positions with both Odell and Quetemoor, but he preferred to stay unattached.<ref name="C&TCW" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|If the heroes see Mazumoda heading their way, they'd better start running.|Daniel Wallace|<ref name="C&TCW" />}}
The Worrite species was featured in ''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'', a reference book published in [[2003]]. The book, a supplement for [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'', features the character Mazumoda in its chapter devoted to Coruscant,<ref name="C&TCW" /> which was written by [[Daniel Wallace]].<ref name="Wizards">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030227chat|text=Developer's Chat: ''Coruscant and the Core Worlds''}}</ref> However, the book provides no statistics for the Worrites in general, so the species is not available for players to use for their own characters.<ref name="C&TCW" />
